formatted_summary:
agent2 (Round 2 summarized): There is currently no clinically supported evidence indicating that isradipine slows the progression of early Parkinson disease; existing research focuses on dopaminergic therapies rather than calcium channel modulators like isradipine for neuroprotection. 

\boxed{B} The provided information confirms lack of efficacy of isradipine in modifying Parkinson disease progression due to absence of supporting clinical data related to its mechanism within this context.  

\boxed{B}

The available scientific literature shows no indication that isradipine affects the progression of early Parkinson disease, even though it involves calcium signaling pathways theoretically linked to neuron function. No trials have demonstrated benefit from isradipine regarding parkinsonian symptom control or delay of motor decline compared to standard care involving dopamine-based agents.

\boxed{B}. Answer choice: B
